body_txt: A specific variation of What Measure Is a Non-Cute? , in which a prehistoric or otherwise extinct animal which is portrayed as more powerful, dangerous and/or deadly (if not also dumber ) than any still-living equivalent to the point where it ceases to be an animal and becomes a marauding monster — larger and faster than any extant apex predator, with no equal in the modern world, filled to the brim with claws and jaws, able to smash apart man-made vehicles to get at the tasty, tasty humans inside, a feat no lion or tiger or bear can do. This is often accompanied with exaggerated features , impossibly large size or hyper-aggressive behavior . This trope is rooted in historic perceptions of prehistory as an unformed, savage, dangerous time, when creatures were brimming with vitality and power but lacked the intelligence or sociality of more "developed" animals. This also tends to affect which animals are depicted this way and how — as a general rule, dinosaurs and older forms of life are more likely to be protrayed as dangerous rampaging monsters than Cenozoic birds and mammals are. If a Fossil Revival is undertaken, this may also lead to the resurrected primordial terrors proving too powerful and savage for modern, more modest life to overmatch. The primary exception to this trend are prehistoric humans , as prothumans and apemen are also likely be depicted as being bigger, stronger, and more savage and violent than modern humanity (see Frazetta Man for those). In all fairness , this trope does have a grain of Truth in Television — to fulfill their status as apex predator, a predator would have to be big or at least strong enough to take down things bigger than itself, often armored or just as aggressive. Most modern-day (land) creatures are nowhere as large as a Brachiosaurus , or as armored as a Triceratops — and without such niches, there's no reason for modern-day animals to be just as enormous or well-armed as a Tyrannosaurus rex . Of course, it bears keeping in mind that most prehistoric critters didn't get any bigger or more formidable than contemporary animals, and even the ones that did were still animals rather than monsters. Classic Carnosaurs , Headbutting Pachy , Raptor Attack , Savage Spinosaurs , Temper-Ceratops , Terrifying Tyrannosaur , Terror-dactyl , and Tough Armored Dinosaur are dinosaurian subtropes . See also Dinosaurs Are Dragons , Dumb Dinos , Reptiles Are Abhorrent , Everything Is Trying to Kill You , Our Monsters Are Different , Primate Versus Reptile , T. Rexpy , Spinosaurus Versus T. rex , Big Creepy-Crawlies , and Historical Villain Upgrade . Meek Mesozoic Mammal is a related concept, where they're contrasted against the "monstrous" dinosaurs. These creatures are common sights in the Age of Reptiles and in Hollywood Prehistory . If you want to see some Real Life information about extinct critters, see here and here . For a popular way of averting this, see Domesticated Dinosaurs , Goofy Feathered Dinosaur , and Non-Malicious Monster . This trope had a heavy influence on the more fantastical Kaiju trope as well, since many early examples are based on dinosaurs or otherwise prehistoric in nature, and in Prehistoric Animal Analogue , as the fascination and vision of extinct animals as more powerful and aggressive led them to be used as inspiration for several fictional designs.
